}}This article details the [[Bradford Bulls]] rugby league football club's 2018 season. This is the Bulls 1st season in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. Following liquidation, the Bulls finished last place in the [[2017 RFL Championship]] season and were relegated alongside [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].

==Season review==

===August 2017===

By the start of August it was confirmed that the Bulls would be relegated into [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] after finishing bottom of the [[2017 RFL Championship]] table.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15457030.Toovey__Bulls_will_battle_to_the_end_____despite_relegation/|title=Toovey: Bulls will battle to the end despite relegation|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=6 August 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Preparation for the 2018 season began in August when it was announced that academy graduate and first team player [[Keenan Tomlinson]] agreed to leave the Bulls by mutual consent. This was swiftly followed by the news that home grown second-rower and the 2017 Player of the Year [[James Bentley (rugby league)|James Bentley]], had signed a 3-year deal with [[Super League]] side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15483318.Bentley__I_can_make_the_step_up_with_Saints/|title=Bulls prospect James Bentley: I can make the step up with St Helens|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=19 August 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref>

===September 2017===

Home grown centre [[Ross Oakes]] and prop forward [[Liam Kirk]] both re-signed with Bradford among speculation that the duo would leave due to the Bulls relegation, however both signed new 2-year deals.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15533337.Bulls_re_sign_Academy_duo_Kirk_and_Oakes/|title=Bradford Bulls re-sign Academy duo Liam Kirk and Ross Oakes|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> It was announced that centre [[James Mendieka]] had been released by the club after three seasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15538738.Bulls__Mendeika_seeking_inner_strength_as_he_embarks_on_next_stage_of_career/|title=Released Bradford Bulls winger James Mendeika seeking inner strength as he embarks on next stage of his career|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=16 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Young academy graduate hooker Joe Lumb signed a 1-year deal with local [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] side [[Keighley Cougars]] after not featuring much during 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15550095.Keighley_Cougars_snap_up_Joe_Lumb_from_Bradford_Bulls/|title=Keighley Cougars snap up Joe Lumb from Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Further departures were announced as winger [[Johnny Campbell (rugby league)|Johnny Campbell]] resigned with his old club [[Batley Bulldogs]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15557689.Jonny_Campbell_leaves_Bradford_Bulls_for_Batley_Bulldogs/|title=Jonny Campbell leaves Bradford Bulls for Batley Bulldogs|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> While fellow winger [[Omari Caro]] announced on social media that he had retired from the sport.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.instagram.com/p/BZJ517Qglsi/|title=Omari Caro announces retirement|work=Instagram |date=17 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Second row forward [[Colton Roche]] joined the mini exodus as he signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] of the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.totalrl.com/huddersfield-sign-bradford-star-2018/|title=Huddersfield sign Bradford star for 2018|work=Total RL |date=29 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Some good news followed the exodus as it was revealed that highly rated academy prospects [[Reiss Butterworth]], [[Evan Hodgson]] and [[Oliver Wilson (rugby league)|Oliver Wilson]] all signed new 3-year contracts with the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15538992.Bulls_snap_up_young_trio_for_future/|title=Bradford Bulls snap up young trio Reiss Butterworth, Evan Hodgson and Olly Wilson for future|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=16 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Hodgson and Butterworth have both featured in the first team during the 2017 season, however Wilson is yet to make his debut. In a shock move, Bradford managed to acquire the services of current superstar halfback [[Dane Chisholm]] for another 2 season despite interest from several other clubs.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15540730.Chisholm_signs_two_year_contract_extension_with_Bulls/|title=Dane Chisholm signs two-year contract extension with Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=17 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Meanwhile, prop forward [[Ross Peltier]] put pen to paper on a new 2-year deal and young halfback [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]] also signed up for another 2 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15544885.Bulls_fan_Peltier_pens__easy__deal_for_more/|title=Bradford Bulls fan Ross Peltier pens 'easy' deal for more|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15553361.Keyes_signs_up_for_two_more_years_at_Bulls/|title=Joe Keyes signs up for two more years at Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=23 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Hooker [[Vila Halafihi]] became the 9th player to re-sign with the Bulls after agreeing a new 2-year deal to stay at Odsal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15558973.Halafihi_glad_to_be_centre_of_attention_with_new_Bulls_deal/|title=Vila Halafihi glad to be centre of attention with new Bradford Bulls deal|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Following this, hooker [[Scott Moore (rugby league)|Scott Moore]] was released after declining to sign a new contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15561433.Hooker_Moore_moving_on_in_Bulls_rebuild/|title=Hooker Scott Moore moving on in Bradford Bulls rebuild|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=28 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> Lifelong Bulls fan and loose forward [[Elliot Minchella]] became the first new signing of the 2018 season as he signed a 2-year part-time deal from [[RFL Championship]] side [[Sheffield Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15550646.New_Bulls_signing_Minchella_comes_back_home_at_last/|title=Bradford Bulls signing Elliot Minchella comes back home at last as he signs two-year deal|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=22 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref> The month ended on a high for the Bulls as they announced the capture of experienced second rower [[Matt Garside]] from Championship team [[London Broncos]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15567685.Garside_signs_up_as_latest_Bulls_addition/|title=Matthew Garside signs up as latest Bradford Bulls addition|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=30 September 2017 |access-date=3 October 2017}}</ref>

===October 2017===

October started with the re-signing of two young academy products, winger [[Josh Rickett]] and fullback [[Brandon Pickersgill]] both signed 2 year part-time deals after making a handful of appearances last season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15573881.Rickett__Bulls_can_make_it_short_and_sweet_in_League_One/?ref=mr&lp=24|title=Rickett: Bulls can make it short and sweet in League One|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 October 2017 |access-date=4 October 2017}}</ref> After initially signing for [[Leigh Centurions]], young winger [[Ethan Ryan]] re-signed with Bradford on a 2-year deal following Leigh's relegation to the Championship.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15581720.Ryan_ready_for_showtime_after_sticking_with_Bulls/|title=Ethan Ryan ready for showtime after sticking with Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 October 2017 |access-date=15 October 2017}}</ref> Prop forward [[James Davies (rugby league)|James Davies]] signed a 1-year extension to his contract to stay at Bradford, whilst it was announced that young prop [[Mikey Wood]] signed on a 1-year loan deal from [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15595388.Potential_coaches_get_in_early_with_Bulls_interest/|title=Potential coaches get in early with Bradford Bulls interest|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 October 2017 |access-date=15 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15597237.Wood_drops_down_to_step_up_with_Bulls/?ref=mr&lp=15|title=Mikey Wood drops down to step up with Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=15 October 2017 |access-date=15 October 2017}}</ref> Young prop [[George Milton (rugby league)|George Milton]] signed a 2-year deal with Bradford from newly promoted side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15608884.High_quality_set_up_attracts_George_Milton_to_Bradford_Bulls/|title=High quality set-up attracts George Milton to Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 October 2017 |access-date=20 October 2017}}</ref> It was announced that former prop and academy graduate [[Steve Crossley]] would return to the club for a third spell from Canadian side [[Toronto Wolfpack]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15622607.Steve_Crossley_back_at_Bradford_Bulls_for_third_spell/|title=Steve Crossley back at Bradford Bulls for third spell|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 October 2017 |access-date=27 October 2017}}</ref>

===November 2017===

Off-contract winger [[Iliess Macani]] departed the Bulls and signed for [[RFL Championship]] side [[Sheffield Eagles]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50421/eagles-make-macani-third-new-signing-ahead-of-|title=EAGLES MAKE MACANI THIRD NEW SIGNING AHEAD OF 2018|work=Sheffield Eagles|date=6 November 2017|access-date=7 November 2017|archive-date=10 November 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171110061800/http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50421/eagles-make-macani-third-new-signing-ahead-of-|url-status=dead}}</ref> It was announced that the Bulls would play local rivals [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in a festive Boxing Day friendly, the first the Bulls have participated in since 2012.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50434/halifax-rlfc-boxing-day-preseason-clash-confirmed-|title=HALIFAX RLFC BOXING DAY PRESEASON CLASH CONFIRMED|work=Bradford Bulls |date=8 November 2017 |access-date=8 November 2017}}</ref> Following this it was also confirmed that the Bulls would play [[Sheffield Eagles]] in a friendly as a part of the [[Dane Chisholm]] transfer midway through last season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/49652/dane-chisholm-released-with-immediate-effect|title=DANE CHISHOLM RELEASED WITH IMMEDIATE EFFECT|work=Sheffield Eagles |date=24 May 2017 |access-date=9 November 2017}}</ref> Young second-row forward [[Liam Johnson]] followed the footsteps of [[Mikey Wood]] and joined the Bulls from [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a season long loan.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15657539.Bulls_sign_Huddersfield_forward_Johnson_on_loan_for_season/|title=Liam Johnson joins Bulls after spells with Sheffield and Oldham|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 November 2017 |access-date=15 November 2017}}</ref> The fixtures for the 2018 season were announced and the Bulls would kick off the campaign away to [[York City Knights]] on 18 February, before hosting the [[London Skolars]] at home on 4 March. The highly anticipated derby with local rivals [[Keighley Cougars]] were scheduled on 11 March (away) and 2 September (home).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50471/-fixtures-announced-|title=2018 FIXTURES ANNOUNCED|work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 November 2017 |access-date=15 November 2017}}</ref> It was also revealed that head coach [[Geoff Toovey]] would not be returning to the Bulls and in the meantime Leigh Beattie will take up the interim head coach role until a new coach is appointed.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50466/club-statement---geoff-toovey-|title=CLUB STATEMENT - GEOFF TOOVEY|work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 November 2017 |access-date=15 November 2017}}</ref> Championship side [[Dewsbury Rams]] released details to two friendlies, with one being against the Bulls on 21 January at [[Crown Flatt]], meanwhile Bradford announced that they would face [[Keighley Cougars]] in a friendly match on 11 February for the Joe Philips Memorial trophy.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://dewsburyrams.co.uk/2017/11/18/rams-announce-two-pre-season-games/|title=Rams announce two pre-season games|work=Dewsbury Rams |date=18 November 2017 |access-date=20 November 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50488/bulls-to-host-keighley-cougars-in-joe-phillips-memorial-fixture|title=BULLS TO HOST KEIGHLEY COUGARS IN JOE PHILLIPS MEMORIAL FIXTURE|work=Bradford Bulls |date=20 November 2017 |access-date=20 November 2017}}</ref> [[Sheffield Eagles]] announced that they would face Bradford in a friendly in 2018 as part of the deal which brought superstar halfback [[Dane Chisholm]] to the Bulls in 2017, the tie will be played on 11 January.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50490/eagles-to-face-bradford-in-pre-season-friendly|title=EAGLES TO FACE BRADFORD IN PRE-SEASON FRIENDLY|work=Sheffield Eagles |date=20 November 2017 |access-date=22 November 2017}}</ref> The newest team in rugby league, [[Toronto Wolfpack]] also announced a ground breaking Transatlantic Challenge which would see them face the Bulls in a pre-season match in 2018 and 2019, with the 2018 fixture being played at Odsal on 26 January and the 2019 fixture likely to be played in [[Canada]] or the [[United States]] to promote the sport.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15683151.Bradford_Bulls_and_Toronto_Wolfpack_launch_Transatlantic_Challenge/?ref=mr&lp=40|title=Bradford Bulls and Toronto Wolfpack launch Transatlantic Challenge|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=24 November 2017 |access-date=28 November 2017}}</ref> Prop forward [[Jon Magrin]] who featured for the Bulls 25 times in 2017 decided to sign with Championship side [[Sheffield Eagles]] on a 2-year deal joining fellow former teammate Iliess Macani.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50492/jamaican-international-magrin-signs-for-sheffield|title=JAMAICAN INTERNATIONAL MAGRIN SIGNS FOR SHEFFIELD|work=Sheffield Eagles |date=29 November 2017 |access-date=30 November 2017}}</ref>

===December 2017===

December started off with the news that the Bulls had signed young utility forward [[Jamel Goodall]] on a one-year contract from [[Castleford Tigers]] after impressing beforehand with [[Keighley Cougars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15708851.Bulls_sign_Goodall_for_2018_season/|title=Bradford Bulls sign utility forward Jamel Goodall for 2018 season|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 December 2017 |access-date=11 December 2017}}</ref> It was also announced that following the departure of [[Geoff Toovey]], former [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Hull F.C.]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] coach [[John Kear]] had signed a 3-year deal to become head coach. His first move was to appoint long term clubman and interim coach [[Leigh Beattie]] as his assistant coach whilst making experienced back [[Lee Smith (rugby)|Lee Smith]] the club captain.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15714857.Bradford_Bulls_set_to_announce_John_Kear_as_new_coach/|title=Bradford Bulls set to announce John Kear as new coach|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 December 2017 |access-date=14 December 2017}}</ref> Bradford also secured the services of young centre/second row [[Callum Bustin]] on a season long loan deal from Castleford Tigers, he has had previous loan spells at fellow [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] clubs [[Newcastle Thunder]] and [[London Skolars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15774659.Callum_Bustin_joins_Bradford_Bulls_on_loan/|title=Callum Bustin joins Bradford Bulls on loan|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 December 2017 |access-date=15 December 2017}}</ref> The Bulls released their new 2018 home shirt which pays tribute to the 40th anniversary of Bradford Northern's Premiership victory in 1978, featuring a red, amber and black chevron on a white background. During this reveal the 2018 squad numbers were also released with club captain Lee Smith retaining the number 1 shirt, other major movers include centre [[Ashley Gibson]] at number 3, [[Sam Hallas]] at number 9, [[Steve Crossley]] at number 10 whilst newcomers [[Matt Garside]], [[Elliot Minchella]] and [[George Milton (rugby league)|George Milton]] will wear the number 11, 12 and 13 shirts.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15778217.Bulls_go_for_iconic_Northern_chevron_in_2018_home_shirt/|title=Bradford Bulls go for iconic Northern chevron in 2018 home shirt|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=17 December 2017 |access-date=18 December 2017}}</ref> With the appointment of the shirt numbers it means that Australian prop [[Damien Sironen]] has not signed a new deal, veteran centre [[Willie Tonga]] and fullback [[Oscar Thomas]] were released. Another new signing was unveiled in the form of experienced Championship winger [[Dalton Grant (rugby league)|Dalton Grant]] on a 1-year deal from [[London Broncos]], Grant missed the 2017 season due to injury but is looking to earn a starting spot for the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50596/bradford-sign-dalton-grant-on-a-one-year-deal|title=BRADFORD SIGN DALTON GRANT ON A ONE YEAR DEAL|work=Bradford Bulls |date=19 December 2017 |access-date=19 December 2017}}</ref> 30 year old former [[Super League]] winger [[Alex Brown (rugby league)|Alex Brown]] signed a 1-year deal with the Bulls from local side [[Batley Bulldogs]] becoming the 10th new recruit for the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50622/alex-brown-snapped-up-by-bulls-|title=ALEX BROWN SNAPPED UP BY BULLS|work=Bradford Bulls |date=22 December 2017 |access-date=23 December 2017}}</ref> The Boxing Day friendly ended with a 22-6 victory for the Bulls with newcomer [[Elliot Minchella]] scoring two tries and fellow newcomer [[Dalton Grant (rugby league)|Dalton Grant]] also crossing for a try whilst youngster [[Liam Kirk]] also scored. Club captain [[Lee Smith (rugby)|Lee Smith]] kicked two goals and academy player [[Rowan Milnes]] also kicked a goal.

===January 2018===

Top points scorer from last season [[Oscar Thomas]] followed former Bulls [[Iliess Macani]] and [[Jon Magrin]] by signing with [[RFL Championship]] side [[Sheffield Eagles]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15821997.Former_Bull_Oscar_Thomas_joins_Sheffield/?ref=mr&lp=24|title=Bradford Bulls: Thomas returns to Odsal for Eagles debut|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 January 2018 |access-date=11 January 2018}}</ref> The next friendly for the Bulls saw them overcome [[Championship (rugby league)|RFL Championship]] side [[Sheffield Eagles]] 12-6 in a very defensive match which saw [[Steve Crossley]] and [[Dalton Grant (rugby league)|Dalton Grant]] claim tried for Bradford whilst youngster [[Rowan Milnes]] kicked two goals. The following game saw the Bulls field a young side away at [[Dewsbury Rams]] for former player [[Paul Sykes (rugby league)|Paul Sykes]]s' testimonial. The Bulls lost the match 24-0. During a press conference it was revealed that the Bulls had entered a dual registration with Championship side [[Toronto Wolfpack]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/50749/bulls-announce-dual-registration-with-toronto-wolfpack-|title=BULLS ANNOUNCE DUAL REGISTRATION WITH TORONTO WOLFPACK|work=Bradford Bulls |date=22 January 2018 |access-date=22 January 2018}}</ref> Also in this press conference the Bulls announced the signing of fullback [[Gregg McNally]] on a two-year deal from [[Leigh Centurions]] and also revealed the new red 2018 away kit.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15888959.Bulls_sign_Leigh_full_back_Gregg_McNally/|title=Bulls sign Leigh full back Gregg McNally|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=22 January 2018 |access-date=22 January 2018}}</ref> The Bulls faced their new dual registration partners in a friendly, however the Toronto side outclassed the Bradford side who lost the match 34-0.

===February 2018===

Recently signed prop forward [[Jamel Goodall]] put pen to paper on a contract extension, after impressing coach [[John Kear]] in the pre-season games, keeping Goodall at the Bulls until after the 2019 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/15917606.Excited_Goodall_takes_long_term_view_with_Bulls/?ref=mr&lp=33|title=Bradford Bulls: Utility forward Jamel Goodall excited after signing new deal to 2019|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=3 February 2018 |access-date=4 February 2018}}</ref> The Bulls finished their pre-season friendlies with a hard-fought 28-20 win over local rivals [[Keighley Cougars]]. Loan signing [[Liam Johnson]] scored a brace whilst [[Alex Brown (rugby league)|Alex Brown]], [[Elliot Minchella]] and [[Brandon Pickersgill]] also scored with [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]] kicking 4 goals. The league season started with a narrow 22-20 victory over promotion rivals [[York City Knights]], prop forward [[Steve Crossley]], centre [[Ashley Gibson]] and Minchella all crossed for a try and Keyes held his nerve in the final minute to kick a penalty goal to provide the win for the Bulls and bring his personal tally to 5 goals for the game. The Bulls announced the signing of Bradford born hooker [[George Flanagan]] from [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/16040287.New_signing_Flanagan_is_back_with_Bradford_Bulls/|title=Bradford Bulls: Free-scoring hooker returns to Odsal after ten-year absence|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=22 February 2018 |access-date=23 February 2018}}</ref> Bradford progressed in the [[2018 Challenge Cup]] with a comprehensive 82-6 win over [[West Wales Raiders]], second rower Minchella scored five tries in the rout with Gibson, Johnson and [[Gregg McNally]] scoring two tries each. [[Callum Bustin]], [[Dane Chisholm]], Crossley and [[Ethan Ryan]] also crossed the try line and Joe Keyes rounded off the scoring with 11 goals.

===March 2018===

March started with the news that trialist [[Harvey Burnett]] had been signed on a permanent deal following an impressive pre-season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/16068876.Bulls_will_keep_cool_at_Keighley__promises_Peltier/|title=Bradford Bulls: Ross Peltier confident John Kear's side won't be dragged into derby brawl|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 March 2018 |access-date=11 March 2018}}</ref> The scheduled league match against [[London Skolars]] was postponed due to bad weather. In their next league match the Bulls came out 54-6 winners over local rivals [[Keighley Cougars]]. New signing [[George Flanagan (rugby league)|George Flanagan]] and [[Elliot Minchella]] both scored a brace of tries whilst [[Dalton Grant (rugby league)|Dalton Grant]], [[Sam Hallas]], [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]], [[Ross Peltier]] and [[Ethan Ryan]] also scored tries, Keyes kicked 9 conversions. Trialist [[Dan King (rugby league)|Dan King]] was allocated the number 16 shirt whilst winger [[Alex Brown (rugby league)|Alex Brown]] retired from the sport to pursue a full-time job. Trialist [[Jordan Andrade]] was also signed on a permanent deal whilst [[Toronto Wolfpack]] centre [[Jake Butler-Fleming]] joined the Bulls on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/16092585.Bulls_snap_up_Butler_Fleming_from_Toronto/|title=Bradford Bulls: Utility back Butler-Fleming signs on loan|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=16 March 2018 |access-date=18 March 2018}}</ref> Prop forward [[James Davies (rugby league)|James Davies]] joined local rivals [[Keighley Cougars]] on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/rlkeighleycougars/16100265.Bradford_Bulls_prop_James_Davies_joins_Keighley_Cougars_on_loan/|title=Bradford Bulls prop James Davies joins Keighley Cougars on loan|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 March 2018 |access-date=21 March 2018}}</ref> Following the win against Keighley the Bulls secured a 32-12 victory over [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]] with Dalton Grant scoring a hat-trick, [[Sam Hallas]] scoring two tries and halfback [[Dane Chisholm]] rounding off the scoring with Keyes making 4 conversions. The Bulls rounded off March with a 30-12 win over fellow promotion contenders [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]], Grant continued his scoring run with a brace of tries whilst Chisholm and Minchella also crossed the line with conversions from Keyes, [[Lee Smith (rugby)|Lee Smith]] and Chisholm.

===April 2018===

The month started with a 34-10 win away at [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]] in the re-arranged [[2018 Challenge Cup]] tie. [[Matt Garside]] scored for the first time in a Bulls shirt whilst [[Liam Johnson]], [[Josh Rickett]] and [[Ethan Ryan]] also scored with [[Elliot Minchella]] scoring his 10th try of the season to ensure the Bulls progressed to the 5th Round and a tie against [[Super League]] side [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/16132876.Great_Dane_helps_kick_Bradford_Bulls_into_next_round/|title=Great Dane helps kick Bradford Bulls into next round|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=3 April 2018 |access-date=13 April 2018}}</ref> However a trip to Cumbria to face former player [[Leon Pryce]]'s side [[Workington Town]] brought the Bulls back to earth as their unbeaten start to the season ended with a 16-17 loss, [[Jake Butler-Fleming]] and [[Ross Peltier]] provided the tries for Bradford. Following the defeat it was announced that [[Jake Butler-Fleming]] would move back to [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in order to facilitate a move to [[York City Knights]], meanwhile the Bulls announced that [[Jy Hitchcox]] from [[Castleford Tigers]] had joined on a month's loan alongside second rower [[James Laithwaite]] who is also on a month's loan from Toronto.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16155987.Bulls_snap_up_Hitchcox_and_Laithwaite/|title=Bulls snap up Hitchcox and Laithwaite|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=12 April 2018 |access-date=13 April 2018}}</ref> The Bulls got back to winning ways with a 52-6 victory over [[Coventry Bears]], new loanee Jy Hitchcox made his mark with two tries whilst Liam Johnson and [[George Flanagan (rugby league)|George Flanagan]] also scored two each. [[Ashley Gibson]], [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]], Elliot Minchella and Ethan Ryan rounded off the scoring. Bradford's [[Challenge Cup]] hopes were ended by Warrington in a 54-6 defeat with a try and goal coming from Joe Keyes. The month ended with a top of the table clash against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], the Bulls came away with a 32-6 victory as Elliot Minchella grabbed a brace with Flanagan, Ryan and [[Mikey Wood]] also scoring.

===May 2018===

The Bulls posted a club record score of 124-0 against [[West Wales Raiders]] to go back to the top of the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16208505.Record-breaking_Bulls_power_to_124-0_win/|title=Record-breaking Bulls power to 124-0 win|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 May 2018 |access-date=25 May 2018}}</ref> [[Elliot Minchella]] and [[Matt Garside]] both scored hat-tricks whilst [[Dane Chisholm]], [[Dalton Grant (rugby league)|Dalton Grant]], [[Brandon Pickersgill]] and [[Ethan Ryan]] scored two tries each. Further tries from [[Callum Bustin]], [[George Flanagan (rugby league)|George Flanagan]], [[Vila Halafihi]], [[Sam Hallas]], [[James Laithwaite]], [[Ross Peltier]] and [[Lee Smith (rugby)|Lee Smith]] ensured the Bulls came away with a heavy win. During the match Chisholm broke the club record for points scored in a match by coming away with 48 points and also broke the goal kicking record by nailing 20 out of 21 conversions. Another big win came in the rearranged league fixture against [[London Skolars]]. Bradford won the match 54-16, Ethan Ryan scoring four tries and fellow wing man Grant grabbing two. Bustin, [[Ashley Gibson]], Peltier, Pickersgill and Smith also scored tries. A tough test followed this as the Bulls came away from [[Newcastle Thunder]] with a narrow 26-16 win. Chisholm, Flanagan, Minchella and Ryan all got on the scoresheet. Meanwhile, youngster [[Reiss Butterworth]] joined [[RFL Championship]] side [[Batley Bulldogs]] on loan for a month.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/16249034.Bulls_welcome_break_after_intense_run/|title=Bradford Bulls: Kear says Easter double took toll|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=25 May 2018 |access-date=25 May 2018}}</ref> The Bulls finished May with a comprehensive 68-0 win over [[Hemel Stags]], Dalton Grant and Elliot Minchella grabbed the headlines scoring a brace of tries each while Chisholm, Flanagan, Garside, Halafihi, Laithwaite, Peltier, Pickersgill and Ryan also scored. Chisholm continued his fine form with the boot kicking 10 from 12.

===June 2018===

June started with a 50-12 win against [[North Wales Crusaders]]. Matt Garside, Ethan Ryan and Lee Smith scored two tries, Dane Chisholm, Joe Keyes and Brandon Pickersgill also scored tries with Chisholm kicking 7 goals.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16280439.full-time-bradford-bulls-50-north-wales-crusaders-12/|title=FULL TIME: Bradford Bulls 50 North Wales Crusaders 12|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 June 2018 |access-date=17 August 2018}}</ref>

==League 1 Playoff Final==
After finishing 2nd in the [[2018 RFL League 1]] season and beating [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] in the Semi Final the Bulls qualified for the League 1 Playoff Final, [[Workington Town]] finished 4th in the table and beat [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] in the Semi Final meaning that the two teams would meet in a play-off match to determine who would be promoted to the [[RFL Championship]] in 2019.
